issue.title: Failed/paused issues indistinguishable from review-pending in kanban; review→execute drag always replays /tiki:yolo

successCriteria.description: Dragging a card whose underlying work status is 'paused' from Review to Execute invokes `/tiki:execute {N} --continue` (resumes from the current phase) instead of `/tiki:yolo`
Dragging a card whose underlying work status is 'failed' from Review to Execute invokes `/tiki:execute {N} --continue` (retry from the failed phase) instead of `/tiki:yolo`
Dragging a card whose underlying work status is 'reviewing' or 'pending' from Review to Execute still invokes `/tiki:yolo {N}` (no behavior change)
Cards in the Review column whose underlying work status is 'failed' or 'paused' are visually differentiated from pending/reviewing cards via a status badge or color modifier
pnpm build from the repo root passes cleanly (`tsc -b` strict mode)

phases.title: Branch review→execute command on underlying work status
phases.content:
Update `apps/desktop/src/components/kanban/KanbanBoard.tsx` so the review→execute drag dispatches the right command based on the issue's Tiki work status.

Design:
1. Update `getExecuteCommand(issueNumber, fromColumn, status?)` to accept an optional underlying work status. When `fromColumn === 'review'` and `status === 'paused' || status === 'failed'`, return `/tiki:execute {N} --continue`. When `fromColumn === 'review'` and the status is `reviewing` / `pending` / undefined, keep the existing `/tiki:yolo {N}` behavior. When `fromColumn === 'open'`, keep `/tiki:yolo`. For any other column (plan etc.) keep the bare `/tiki:execute {N}` behavior.
2. Update `triggerExecution(issueNumber, fromColumn)` to look up `activeWork[\`issue:${issueNumber}\`]?.status` (narrowed to `IssueWork`) and pass it through to `getExecuteCommand`. Use the existing `activeWork` selector that is already in scope at the top of the component.
3. The `handleDragEnd` review→execute branch already calls `triggerExecution`, so no signature change is needed at the call site.

Rationale: paused/failed work has plan + partial phase progress in `.tiki/state.json`. Re-running `/tiki:yolo` would re-fetch, re-review, re-plan and overwrite that state. `/tiki:execute --continue` is the correct resume entry point.
verification: getExecuteCommand returns `/tiki:execute {N} --continue` when fromColumn is 'review' and status is 'paused'
getExecuteCommand returns `/tiki:execute {N} --continue` when fromColumn is 'review' and status is 'failed'
getExecuteCommand returns `/tiki:yolo {N}` when fromColumn is 'review' and status is 'reviewing' / 'pending' / undefined
triggerExecution reads activeWork[`issue:${N}`]?.status and threads it into getExecuteCommand

phases.title: Visual differentiation for failed/paused cards in the Review column
phases.content:
Confirm and harden the visual treatment of paused/failed cards.

`apps/desktop/src/components/kanban/KanbanCard.tsx` already renders a corner badge (`.kanban-card-badge--paused` ⏸ / `.kanban-card-badge--failed` !) and applies `kanban-card--paused` / `kanban-card--failed` modifiers when `workItem.status` matches. `apps/desktop/src/components/kanban/kanban.css` already declares the modifiers and badge color rules. The reason failed/paused cards in the Review column appear identical to pending cards in practice is that the badge color alone (single character, transparent background) is too subtle to read at a glance.

Design:
1. In `kanban.css`, upgrade `.kanban-card-badge--paused` and `.kanban-card-badge--failed` from bare-color text to small pill badges that match the WorkProgressCard pattern (`background` + `color` pair with translucent fill, `padding: 1px 5px`, `border-radius: 3px`, uppercase 9px text). Use the same color palette already in use: amber `#f59e0b` for paused, red `#ef4444` for failed.
2. Update the badge content in `KanbanCard.tsx` from the single character glyphs to short uppercase text labels (`PAUSED`, `FAILED`) so they are immediately readable. Keep the existing `kanban-card--paused` / `kanban-card--failed` left-border modifier — the combination of border + pill is the visible differentiator.
3. No new components, no animations.

Rationale: matches the existing WorkProgressCard status-pill pattern (`.work-progress-status.failed` etc.) so users see the same status vocabulary across the sidebar and the kanban.
verification: kanban.css `.kanban-card-badge--paused` and `--failed` have background + color + padding + border-radius (pill shape)
KanbanCard.tsx renders 'PAUSED' / 'FAILED' text in the badge instead of the single-character glyph
Visual: in the Review column a failed card has a red pill + red left border; a paused card has an amber pill + amber left border; a pending/reviewing card has neither
